up.method: Max acetaldehyde at 2000 mM ethanol; C1/2 = 964.5 +/- 408.8 mM (highest)

sec.method: Vmax by GC on resting washed cells at 2000 mM ethanol; +/- 8.1

growth_records.cite: Tagaino R et al. Bifacial biological effects of ethanol: acetaldehyde production by oral Streptococcus species and the antibacterial effects of ethanol against these bacteria. Journal of Oral Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.1080/20002297.2021.1937884. [PMC8204988]
growth_records.notes: Resting-cell assay. Acetaldehyde Vmax=19.2 umol/mg wet wt/min (lowest) at 2000 mM ethanol; C1/2=964.5 mM (highest -> needs high ethanol to produce acetaldehyde).
